behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module handoff: request-builder output must be correctly wired into the API client payload. A pure Unit test of one helper can miss wiring/serialization drops between modules. Higher levels are not required: behavior text is only about payload content, not real network/OS effects or model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that records outbound payload; invoke classify with a known taxonomy and assert every valid label is present in the sent request. Also assert no label is omitted when taxonomy size changes. This catches cross-module payload-construction/handoff failures.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic mapping/validation logic on returned label value. Real failure is local decision logic, not infrastructure interaction. Rejected higher levels because behavior text focuses on handling an already-returned invalid label, not transport/retry/model-quality concerns.
behaviors.test_description: Call normalization/validation path with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and raw label 'medical'; assert final output is 'unknown'. Include case-insensitive/whitespace variants if normalization is part of same function.

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: This is pure determinism/idempotence of normalization for identical input. No module boundary, I/O, or model dependency is required to expose the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-based with same fixture reused) on the exact same raw API response object/string and assert output label is identical each time.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live calls and consistency tied to provider output changes. This depends on real AI API behavior/non-determinism and cannot be validated with fixed stubs (they hide drift/variance). Rejected Workflow because this is repeated single-step classification, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated real provider calls for the same document+taxonomy, recording raw provider response and module label each run. Assert: when raw provider response is unchanged between runs, module label is unchanged; label differences are allowed only when provider response differs.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at boundary between token-budget logic and dispatch path: enforcement must occur before send. Unit-only tests of budget math can miss accidental dispatch despite over-budget input. Rejected System/Agentic because behavior text is explicitly pre-dispatch enforcement; no need for real network or model output content.
behaviors.test_description: Wire module with real token-budget component and stub API client. Provide over-budget input and assert policy outcome (truncate or reject) plus dispatch behavior: truncated payload sent within limit, or no dispatch on reject.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires real provider-reported usage; stubs can always return compliant numbers and hide real over-budget behavior. The execution path includes a real AI API call, so minimum should cover real model/provider behavior, not just local wiring. Rejected Workflow because it is a single call measurement, not multi-step agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents to the real provider through the module and read provider-reported token usage from response metadata. Assert reported usage <= configured per-call budget across sample docs.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is orchestration across retry/backoff and client-call modules. A stubbed client returning 429 then success can reproduce it without real network. Rejected System because behavior text is protocol-level handling ('API signals ... too high'), which is reproducible with in-process fake responses; no OS/subprocess dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Use stub provider client that returns rate-limit error (e.g., 429) on first call and success on second; assert module delays/backs off per policy, retries, and returns successful label instead of surfacing initial error.
